audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Peter's Jerusalem explanation, no distinction, Gentile turning to life, Antioch community, Barnabas/Saul, Christian naming, Agabus famine relief.

verses.restored: The apostles and the siblings throughout Judea heard that the nations also received the word of the Divine.
When Peter went up to Jerusalem, those from circumcision were disputing with him,
saying, You went in to men having uncircumcision and ate with them.
Peter began and explained to them in order, saying,
I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision: a certain vessel descending like a great linen sheet, being lowered from heaven by four corners, and it came to me.
Looking intently into it, I observed and saw the four-footed creatures of the earth, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds of heaven.
I also heard a voice saying to me, Rise, Peter; slaughter and eat.
But I said, By no means, Lord, because common or unclean has never entered into my mouth.
A voice answered a second time from heaven, What the Divine cleansed, you must not make common.
This happened three times, and all was drawn up again into heaven.
Look, immediately three men stood at the house in which we were, having been sent to me from Caesarea.
The Spirit told me to go with them, making no distinction. These six siblings also came with me, and we entered into the man's house.
He reported to us how he saw the messenger standing in his house and saying, Send to Joppa and summon Simon called Peter,
who will speak words to you by which you will be saved/healed, you and all your house.
As I began to speak, the holy Spirit fell upon them just as also upon us at the beginning.
I remembered the word of the Lord, how he said, John baptized in water, but you will be baptized in holy Spirit.
If therefore the Divine gave them the equal gift as also to us when we trusted upon the Lord Jesus Messiah, who was I to be able to hinder the Divine?
Hearing these things, they became quiet and glorified the Divine, saying, Then to the nations also the Divine has given turning/realignment into life.
Now those scattered from the pressure that happened over Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, speaking the word to no one except Judeans only.
But there were some of them, men of Cyprus and Cyrene, who came to Antioch and were speaking also to the Greeks, announcing the good news of the Lord Jesus.
The hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number, trusting, turned to the Lord.
The word about them was heard in the ears of the assembly in Jerusalem, and they sent out Barnabas as far as Antioch.
When he came and saw the grace of the Divine, he rejoiced and encouraged all to remain with the Lord with purpose of heart.
For he was a good man and full of holy Spirit and trust; and a considerable crowd was added to the Lord.
He went out to Tarsus to seek Saul.

Finding him, he brought him to Antioch. It happened that for a whole year they were gathered in the assembly and taught a considerable crowd, and in Antioch the disciples were first called Christians.
In these days prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
One from them named Agabus stood up and signified through the Spirit that a great famine was about to be over the whole inhabited world, which happened under Claudius.
According as any of the disciples had means, each determined to send service to the siblings dwelling in Judea.
They did this, sending it to the elders by the hand of Barnabas and Saul.
